tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, elevated creatinine, and significant proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, high protein, ketones, low specific gravity) indicate possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury, but no evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent clinical evaluation for renal impairment and hypoglycemia, with further nephrological and metabolic assessment.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
